title: Millimeter to Inch Converter
description: Industrial-grade conversion tool with precision validation, loss analysis, and confidence-adjusted output for engineering and manufacturing applications.

inputs.label: Value in Millimeters
inputs.businessContext: The linear dimension to be converted from millimeters to inches. Typical range for industrial parts: 0.1 mm to 10,000 mm.

inputs.label: Decimal Precision
inputs.businessContext: Number of decimal places for the inch result. ISO 2768-m standard suggests 2-4 decimals for general tolerances.

inputs.label: Tolerance (mm)
inputs.businessContext: Manufacturing tolerance in millimeters. Used to compute tolerance in inches and assess quality thresholds.

inputs.label: Target Unit System
inputs.businessContext: Select the output unit system. Imperial outputs inches; metric outputs millimeters (for cross-check).

inputs.label: Apply Six Sigma Adjustment
inputs.businessContext: If true, applies a 1.5 sigma shift to account for process drift (Six Sigma DMAIC methodology).

rules.message: Value in millimeters must be non-negative.
Tolerance must be greater than zero.
Precision must be between 0 and 6 decimal places.
Tolerance should not exceed the measured value (except zero).

thresholds.message: Tolerance exceeds 0.5 mm. Consider tightening for precision applications (ISO 2768-f).
Value is less than 1 mm. Ensure measurement instrument resolution is adequate (WERC guideline).
Six Sigma shift applied to large dimension. Verify process capability (Cpk > 1.33).

formulas.name: Raw Inches
formulas.description: Direct conversion from millimeters to inches using 1 inch = 25.4 mm.

formulas.name: Rounded Inches
formulas.description: Raw inches rounded to the specified decimal precision.

formulas.name: Tolerance in Inches
formulas.description: Tolerance converted to inches for quality control.

formulas.name: Upper Specification Limit (inches)
formulas.description: Upper bound of acceptable dimension in inches.

formulas.name: Lower Specification Limit (inches)
formulas.description: Lower bound of acceptable dimension in inches.

formulas.name: Six Sigma Adjusted Inches
formulas.description: Applies a 1.5 sigma shift to the rounded inches if enabled. Shift = 0.001 * value_mm (approximate).

formulas.name: Primary Result (Final Inches)
formulas.description: The final converted value, adjusted for precision and optionally Six Sigma.

primary.label: Converted Value (inches)
primary.description: Final inch value after precision rounding and optional Six Sigma adjustment.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Rounding Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Loss of precision due to rounding to specified decimal places.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Tolerance Bandwidth
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Total tolerance range in inches, representing potential variation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Six Sigma Shift Magnitude
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Magnitude of the shift applied if Six Sigma is enabled.

suggestedActions.action: Review tolerance specification. Consider ISO 2768-m or tighter class for precision fits.
Increase decimal precision to at least 3 for better accuracy in manufacturing drawings.
Perform a process capability study (Cpk) to validate the Six Sigma shift assumption.
Use a micrometer with 0.001 mm resolution to ensure measurement accuracy per WERC guidelines.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Value
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Confidence-adjusted inch value considering rounding and tolerance losses. Uses a simple weighted factor: 1 - (loss_rounding + loss_tolerance/2) / raw_inches, clamped to [0.8, 1.0].
